title: 14 Managing Risk on PaymentStream AFT
body_text:
14	Managing Risk on PaymentStream AFT
14.1	Overview of Managing Risk on PaymentStream AFT
Risk management on
PaymentStreamГўВ„Вў
AFT includes Originator limits and risk monitoring. When an OriginatorГўВҖВҷs set AFT limit is reached, the system sends an email notification to the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs primary email address and any addresses entered in the Additional Emails field.
Financial institutions using the User Management system can manage limits for their Originators and for their business membersГўВҖВҷ Originators. Financial institutions that havenГўВҖВҷt yet implemented User Management can contact
PaymentStream
Support to request changes to the limits for their Originators and for their business membersГўВҖВҷ Originators. See Section 8.3, Requesting Originator Information Changes on PaymentStream AFT, for more information.
Temporary transaction or settlement limits can be raised and lowered on the same day. If the monthly limit is affected by a temporary transaction or settlement limit increase, the monthly limit must be increased and will remain increased until the beginning of the next month.
14.2	Originator Limits
An Originator can have separate limits for debits and separate limits for credits. If the Originator is set up with only one type, the system defaults to that type. Originators are set up with a transaction limit, settlement day limit, and a monthly limit as follows:
Transaction Limit
ГўВҖВ“ The amount of an AFT transaction canГўВҖВҷt exceed the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs transaction limit.
Settlement Day Limit
ГўВҖВ“ The total amount of AFT transactions settled in a day canГўВҖВҷt exceed the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs settlement day limit (transactions due on weekends and holidays will settle on the following business day).
Monthly Limit
ГўВҖВ“ The total amount of AFT transactions settled in a calendar month canГўВҖВҷt exceed the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs monthly limit.
Accumulated Monthly Limit
ГўВҖВ“ Shows the accumulated total within a calendar month, as of that day, by the system.
14.3	Mandatory Approval
Approval of tasks is mandatory for users acting on behalf of Originators whose monthly limits are set to one million dollars or more. The million-dollar calculation considers the accumulative value of monthly limits set for all Originator IDs for an Organization. If either the monthly debits or monthly credits for all combined Originator IDs for a single Organization is one million dollars or more, users acting on behalf of all Originator IDs for this Organization will require approval.
END
